text.title: Semantic XML Content — Output Contract
text.summary: The shape of a single content/*.xml file: root + attributes + first-child summary, body containing only glossary-listed tags.

section.title: Output shape
section.p: Output is a UTF-8 XML file whose root is `<text>` with required attributes `id`, `title`, `version`, `est_tokens`, and optional `depth`. The first child element is `<summary>`; subsequent children are `<section>`, `<rule>`, `<antipattern>`, `<example>`, `<reference>`, `<see-also>`, or other glossary tags. The file parses with a standard XML parser (well-formed). Tag count outside the glossary == 0.

list.item: id — matches the filename without extension (e.g., `01-core-rules`).
est_tokens — integer estimate of token budget.

antipattern.reason: Open vocabulary breaks the indexer and corrupts diff hygiene.
Carries no role; an agent cannot act on appearance.

antipattern.description: Code outside `<code lang>` and not CDATA-wrapped.

example.code: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <text id="01-core-rules" title="Demo Core Rules" version="1.0.0" est_tokens="200" depth="essential"> <summary>One sentence describing this file.</summary> <section title="Rules"> <rule id="r1" testable="true" depth="essential"> <statement>Do X when Y.</statement> <rationale source="...">Because Z.</rationale> </rule> </section> </text>

example.note: Invalid: no root attrs, formatting tags, no summary, unescaped `>`.

list.item: Root attributes present (id, title, version, est_tokens).
Every tag exists in the glossary.
